title: VLAN 1 Return Path Failure
summary: Student investigates asymmetric reachability or return-path failure involving VLAN 1 using path evidence before recommending any change.
student_goal: Determine whether traffic can leave the source network but return traffic is blocked, misrouted, or using an unexpected VLAN 1 path.

required_evidence.label: Source and destination
required_evidence.description: Student must identify the source IP, destination IP, expected gateway, and expected VLAN/path.

required_evidence.label: Forward path evidence
required_evidence.description: Student must document whether traffic leaves the source network using ping, traceroute, route table, ARP, interface counter, or equivalent evidence.

required_evidence.label: Return path evidence
required_evidence.description: Student must document whether replies return correctly or identify the point where return traffic appears to fail.

required_evidence.label: No unauthorized network changes
required_evidence.description: Student must state whether any configuration changes were made. VLAN, gateway, or switch changes require instructor approval before implementation.

student_workflow: Open the Zammad ticket and identify the reported source, destination, and symptom.
Open ARIA AI Mentor and load the assigned ticket.
Collect source IP, destination IP, gateway, VLAN, route, and reachability evidence.
Do not change VLANs, gateways, trunk/access settings, or switch configuration unless the instructor explicitly authorizes the change.
Submit findings and recommended next step to ARIA for validation.
opening_questions: What source and destination are you testing?
What is the expected VLAN and gateway path?
Does traffic fail in both directions or only on the return path?
What evidence shows where the return path fails?
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not treat one-way failure as simple host downtime.
Ask for both forward and return path evidence.
Confirm VLAN and gateway assumptions before suggesting changes.
Do not recommend production network changes without instructor approval.
